turns.user_message: Someone made a really loud, offhand joke about how I never react to anything anymore. It used to just roll right off my shoulders, but today it just sat there. I tried to laugh it off like I normally would, but the words just felt completely hollow. I kept staring at the screen and wondering why my usual responses feel so heavy now. It’s just a weird feeling to watch a familiar moment happen and feel absolutely nothing behind it.

That same kind of comment keeps popping up lately, and it’s starting to wear me down. I used to brush it off without a second thought, but now it just highlights how empty everything feels. I catch myself going through the same daily routines, waiting for a spark that never actually shows up. Even when good things happen around me, it’s like there’s a thick glass wall between me and the moment. I just keep nodding along while everything else fades into the background.

I keep hearing those same remarks echo in my head, and it’s exhausting trying to pretend I’m still the same person. The things that used to bring me joy now just feel like chores I have to complete. I sit through entire days watching everything happen around me while my own reactions stay completely flat. It’s like I’m running on a script that’s slowly losing its meaning, and I can’t find a way to rewrite it. I just want to feel something real again, but everything stays stubbornly out of reach.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something someone else said or did. This used to not bother the speaker.
